data.title: SEC Seeks Public Input on Capital-Raising Policies for Small Businesses
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is inviting public input on ways to enhance capital-raising policies for small businesses. The SEC posted on X, urging individuals to submit their recommendations before the annual Small Business Forum scheduled for March 9. The selected priorities will be presented to Congress. Submissions are due today at noon.

data.title: Ripple Expands Payments Platform with Managed Custody and Virtual-Account Collections
data.description: Ripple has enhanced its payments platform by introducing managed custody and virtual-account collections, aiming to provide an "end-to-end" service. According to NS3.AI, this platform has processed over $100 billion across more than 60 markets. Additionally, Ripple's prime broker, Hidden Road Partners CIV US LLC, has been included in the NSCC MPID directory for its inaugural OTC trade, integrating a Ripple entity into a key U.S. stock-clearing system. These developments align with Ripple's strategy to connect traditional finance, stablecoins, and the XRP Ledger, while maintaining optional usage of XRP.

data.title: Wintermute Receives Significant Bitcoin Transfer
data.description: A substantial transfer of Bitcoin was made to Wintermute, a prominent cryptocurrency trading firm. According to ChainCatcher, data from Arkham indicates that at 23:16, 340 BTC were moved from several anonymous addresses to Wintermute. This transaction highlights ongoing activity in the cryptocurrency market, reflecting the dynamic nature of digital asset exchanges.

data.title: President Trump commits tech giants to cover power costs amid AI data center rush
data.description:
President Donald Trump gathered the leaders of America’s most powerful technology companies at the White House on Wednesday, March 4, 2026, to sign a Ratepayer Protection Pledge. The pledge is a voluntary commitment by the likes of Amazon, Google, Meta, Microsoft, OpenAI, Oracle, and xAI to build, finance, or procure their own power generation and […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/tech-giants-to-cover-power-costs-data-center/

data.title: Azerbaijan Halts Cargo Transit Through Iran Border
data.description: Azerbaijan has announced the suspension of cargo transit through its border with Iran. According to Jin10, this decision comes amid rising tensions between the two countries. The halt in transit is expected to impact trade routes and logistics operations in the region. The reasons behind the suspension have not been disclosed, but it highlights the ongoing geopolitical challenges faced by Azerbaijan and Iran. This development may lead to increased scrutiny on regional trade dynamics and diplomatic relations.

data.title: Opinion: Ethereum's recent pullback is more like a "value reset," and the fundamentals remain solid after the liquidation wave.
data.description: Sam Tabar, CEO of Bit Digital, stated in an article on the X platform that Ethereum's recent pullback was more like a "value reset." The market experienced a rapid revaluation after a significant influx of leverage, with a wave of liquidations leaving clear traces of market correction. The combined pressure from both long and short positions amplified volatility. Now that the market has stabilized, Ethereum's price has returned to the structural support range of $1,800 to $2,100. Currently, Ethereum's fundamentals have not changed substantially: stablecoin settlements are active, rollups remain pegged to the mainnet, and Ethereum remains the preferred choice in programmable settlement scenarios requiring institutional risk vetting. Market bottoms are usually only confirmed in hindsight, but this event demonstrates how leverage can quickly distort market trends, while Ethereum's long-term value and fundamentals remain solid.

data.title: Analyst Argues XRP’s Real Edge Isn’t Price — It’s Neutrality
data.description:
Rather than pitching XRP as a “moonshot” or a BTC rival, the wealth expert frames it as a structurally-neutral bridge asset.
source: https://dailycoin.com/analyst-argues-xrps-real-edge-isnt-price-its-neutrality/?utm_source=cryptopanic&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Son of U.S. Government Contractor Arrested for Alleged Crypto Theft
data.description: John Daghita, the son of a U.S. government contractor, has been arrested by the FBI for allegedly stealing $46 million in cryptocurrency from the U.S. Marshals Service. According to BWEnews, the arrest follows an investigation into the theft, which involved significant amounts of digital currency. The case highlights ongoing concerns about security and integrity within government operations involving cryptocurrency. Further details about the investigation and potential charges against Daghita have not been disclosed.

data.title: Private Credit Faces Scrutiny Amid Default Concerns
data.description: Private credit has become a focal point of discussion, drawing attention for reasons that are not entirely positive. Bloomberg posted on X that the topic was extensively debated at the recent Bloomberg Invest conference. Participants were divided over a contentious prediction of a 15% default rate, yet there was a general consensus that challenges are imminent for the sector.

data.title: Web3 Foundation's strategic adjustment: It will transfer Polkadot governance support, Polkadot Wiki, and other projects.
data.description: The Web3 Foundation has officially announced a strategic shift, returning to its core mission of promoting and managing Web3 resources, supporting community, governance, and technological infrastructure, and focusing more on its long-term vision and resource management. Based on this strategic shift, the Web3 Foundation will transfer several projects and initiatives, including the JAM Prize, Polkadot governance support, the Polkadot Wiki, the knowledge base, Kusama Vision, and developer documentation, to other teams and organizations to ensure long-term impact and a healthy ecosystem.

data.title: Chainlink, in collaboration with Visa and Zhongxia Fund, completed testing of a cross-border foreign exchange settlement solution.
data.description: Visa, ANZ Bank, Zhongxia Fund, and Fidelity International announced with the Hong Kong Monetary Authority that they successfully completed a cross-border foreign exchange settlement solution using Chainlink in the e-HKD project. This solution demonstrates how regulated digital assets can move securely between private and public blockchains, embedding automated compliance and atomic settlement directly into the transaction process. Chainlink's Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) enabled asset transfers between ANZ's private blockchain DASChain and the Ethereum Sepolia testnet.

data.title: Bitcoin Spot ETF Net Flows Turn Positive as Bitcoin Surpasses $70,000
data.description: Bitcoin spot ETF net flows have turned positive again as Bitcoin's price surpassed $70,000 on February 14. According to NS3.AI, this development suggests a reduction in selling pressure. Glassnode reports that ETF flows are stabilizing following a period of sustained outflows, although institutional demand remains uncertain. Despite this uncertainty, there are early indications of accumulation by institutional investors.

data.title: Global Supply Chain Pressure Index Rises in February
data.description: The Global Supply Chain Pressure Index for February increased to 0.49 from the previous value of 0.41. According to Jin10, this rise indicates growing challenges in the global supply chain, which may impact various industries worldwide. The index measures the stress and disruptions within supply chains, reflecting factors such as transportation delays, production bottlenecks, and demand fluctuations. As supply chain issues continue to evolve, businesses and policymakers are closely monitoring these developments to mitigate potential impacts on economic growth and stability.

data.title: U.S. Unemployment Claims Rise Slightly, Labor Market Remains Stable
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Labor reported on March 5 that initial unemployment claims for the week ending February 28 reached 213,000. According to BlockBeats, this figure is higher than the previous count of 212,000 but falls short of the market expectation of 215,000, indicating a generally low level of layoffs in the labor market.

The data suggests that after disruptions from holidays and winter weather, initial unemployment claims have returned to levels near last year's lows. Meanwhile, continuing unemployment claims rose to 1.87 million, marking the largest increase this year.

The Federal Reserve's Beige Book noted that recent employment levels have remained stable, with hiring activity largely unchanged in seven out of twelve districts. Some businesses have reported that rising non-labor costs, weak demand, and economic uncertainty have limited their hiring efforts.

The market is currently anticipating the release of the non-farm payroll report on Friday. Following strong employment data in January, it is widely expected that job growth in February will slow, although the unemployment rate is predicted to remain stable.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Urges Increased Security Investment in Western Hemisphere
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has called on all partners in the Western Hemisphere to enhance their efforts and increase investments in the security sector. According to Jin10, Hegseth emphasized the importance of collaboration among nations in the region to address shared security challenges effectively. The call for increased investment comes amid growing concerns over regional stability and the need for a coordinated approach to security threats. Hegseth's remarks highlight the U.S. commitment to strengthening alliances and ensuring a secure environment in the Western Hemisphere.

data.title: Coinbase will launch OPN perpetual futures contracts.
data.description: Coinbase will launch OPN perpetual futures contracts. The OPN-PERP market will open once liquidity conditions are met and trading is supported in the region.

data.title: The Art of Concise Communication in the Digital Age
data.description: The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X about the evolving nature of communication in the digital era. The post reflects on the past when brevity was a necessity, requiring individuals to convey complex ideas succinctly within character limits. This skill, once essential, is perceived to be diminishing with the rise of lengthy threads and AI-generated essays. The post suggests a nostalgia for the time when concise communication was an art form, highlighting a shift in how information is shared and understood in today's digital landscape.

data.title: Polish Central Bank Governor: Iran Conflict Unlikely to End Soon
data.description: The Governor of Poland's Central Bank has expressed skepticism regarding the swift resolution of the ongoing conflict in Iran. According to Jin10, the governor highlighted the complexities and geopolitical tensions surrounding the situation, suggesting that a quick end to the hostilities is improbable. The remarks come amid heightened global attention on the region, with potential implications for international markets and economic stability. The governor's comments reflect broader concerns about the prolonged nature of the conflict and its impact on global economic conditions.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Criticizes Globalism at Summit
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth expressed concerns over globalism during his speech at the Americas Summit, highlighting it as a potential risk. According to Jin10, Hegseth also criticized diversity initiatives, suggesting they may undermine national interests. His remarks come amid ongoing debates about the impact of globalism on national security and economic stability. The summit, attended by leaders from across the Americas, focused on regional cooperation and security challenges. Hegseth's comments are likely to spark further discussion on the balance between global cooperation and national sovereignty.
